title: A note on potential Type II blowups of axisymmetric solutions to the Navier-Stokes equations

prop:1.1.summary: Under Type II blowup conditions (1.2), (1.3), (1.5), Euler scaling produces a non-trivial ancient solution u to the Euler equations on $R^3$ x (-inf,0) satisfying scaled energy bounds and a local energy inequality.

lem:2.1.summary: Under pointwise decay $|v| \le c/|x'|^\alpha$ and $l_1 \le s_1$, the nonlinear quantity $F(f) = (2/l_1)|\omega_\theta/r|^{l_1/2}$ satisfies a weak transport equation against axisymmetric test functions on $Q_-$.

prop:2.2.summary: Under the conditions of Lemma 2.1 plus restriction (2.10) and finite initial vorticity integral $g(t_0) < \infty$, the integral $g(t) = (2/l_1)$ int $|\omega_\theta(u)/r|^{l_1/2}$ dx is constant in time for all t $\le 0$.

prop:2.3.summary: Under condition (2.10) and $L^q$ integrability of v with q=3/(2-m), the origin can be a Type II singular point only if at least one of conditions (1.3), (2.3), or (2.4) is violated. Proved by showing u(.,0)=0 via Euler scaling, then using Prop 2.2 to get irrotationality, contradicting non-triviality.

cor:2.4.summary: Vorticity version of Prop 2.3: under (2.10) and $L^{q_1}$ integrability of curl v with $q_1=3/(3-m)$, Type II blowup at the origin requires violation of at least one of (1.3), (2.3), or (2.4).

prop:3.1.summary: For self-similar solutions with $\alpha_0=0 (no$ spatial weight) and $3/(\alpha+1) < s_1 < 3/2$, the profile U vanishes identically. Proved by iterating a reverse Sobolev inequality $I(R) \le$ c/R * I(2R) using scaled second-derivative bounds.

prop:4.1.summary: Under the conditions of Proposition 2.2, the discrete self-similar profile U vanishes identically. Proved by combining time-constancy of the vorticity integral (Prop 2.2) with periodicity of U, forcing the integral to zero as t $\to 0-$.

edges.detail: Uses the ancient solution u and its properties (eq 1.6, 1.7) produced by Prop 1.1 via Euler scaling, plus assumption (1.5)
Uses the weak transport identity (2.5) from Lemma 2.1 to derive time-derivative estimates for $g_a(t)$, then takes a $\to \infty$
Uses constancy of vorticity integral (Prop 2.2): after showing u(.,0)=0 via Euler scaling convergence, concludes $\omega=0$, then Prop 2.2 gives irrotationality for all time
Uses the blowup function u from Prop 1.1 and its non-triviality (1.9) to reach contradiction with irrotationality
Vorticity-norm reformulation of Prop 2.3 via standard Sobolev embedding from curl to velocity
Uses the scaled bounds (3.5) on U inherited from the ancient solution u of Prop 1.1
Directly invokes Prop 2.2 to get time-constancy of vorticity integral, then combines with discrete self-similarity periodicity

blowup_extraction.description: Euler scaling extracts a non-trivial ancient Euler solution from a Type II blowup scenario.

transport_machinery.description: Establishes weak transport equation for the nonlinear vorticity quantity F(f) on the half-space $Q_-$.

vorticity_conservation.description: Time-constancy of the $L^{l_1/2}$ norm of $\omega_\theta/r$, the core rigidity mechanism.

blowup_exclusion.description: Main exclusion results: Type II blowup impossible under various integrability conditions on v or curl v.

self_similar_exclusion.description: Vanishing of self-similar and discrete self-similar profiles under the paper's hypotheses.
